2606.24655.title: AI-PAVE-Br: Leveraging Large Language Models for Enhanced Product Attribute Value Extraction through a Golden Set Approach
2606.24655.paper_summary: The explosive growth and complexity of product data within the dynamic Brazilian e-commerce landscape demand robust and specialized methods for structured information extraction. Traditional approaches to Product Attribute Value Extraction (PAVE) often struggle with the linguistic nuances and sheer diversity of product descriptions in Portuguese. To address this critical gap, this paper introduces two major contributions. First, we present AI-PAVEBr, a specialized system engineered with Large Language Models (LLMs) to perform high-accuracy PAVE specifically for Brazilian e-commerce catalogs. Second, to facilitate reproducible research and provide a definitive benchmark, we introduce and share the Golden Set, a new, meticulously curated, and manually annotated dataset for PAVE in Portuguese. We detail the creation process and structure (Entity, Category, Subcategories) of this high-quality reference set. Our experiments conclusively show that AI-PAVE-Br, leveraging targeted prompt engineering, dramatically outperforms conventional Named Entity Recognition (NER) baselines. This work not only delivers a superior, scalable solution for a major non-English market but also enriches the NLP community with a valuable, publicly available resource for future PAVE research.
2606.24655.paper_summary_zh: 爆炸性的增長和巴西電子商務領域內產品數據的複雜性要求強大且專門的方法來進行結構化信息提取。傳統的產品屬性值提取（PAVE）方法常常在葡萄牙語的語言細微差別和產品描述的多樣性上面臨挑戰。為了解決這一關鍵缺口，本文提出了兩項主要貢獻。首先，我們介紹了AI-PAVEBr，一個專門設計的系統，利用大型語言模型（LLMs）來執行針對巴西電子商務目錄的高準確度PAVE。其次，為了促進可重複的研究並提供明確的基準，我們介紹並分享了Golden Set，一個新的、經過精心策劃和手動標註的葡萄牙語PAVE數據集。我們詳細說明了這個高質量參考集的創建過程和結構（實體、類別、子類別）。我們的實驗明確顯示，AI-PAVE-Br利用針對性的提示工程，顯著超越了傳統的命名實體識別（NER）基準。這項工作不僅為一個主要的非英語市場提供了一個優越的可擴展解決方案，還為自然語言處理社區提供了一個有價值的、公開可用的資源，以促進未來的PAVE研究。
2606.24655.author: Murilo Gazzola et.al.
2606.24655.authors: Murilo Gazzola, Hugo Gobato Souto, Samuel Silva, Júlia Schubert Peixoto, Felipe Siqueira, André Luis Pedroso de Morais, Caio Gomes
